ending: Ashlock survives, but only barely and utterly changed. The Dao Storm tears his tree body apart before dissolving into 'Black Rain,' bark fragments that instantly sprout thousands of demonic trees across Red Vine Peak and Darklight City. Reduced to a dying ember, Ashlock is revived when Stella pours her own cultivation energy into his core. Offered a choice by his rebooted system, he permanently merges his human ego into his tree body rather than be reborn as a separate human, losing his discrete star core (his whole trunk now holds his power) and accepting that his human traits will slowly erode. A youthful Senior Lee appears in a vision to explain the wider cosmology and then departs, seemingly for good. Ashlock wakes linked to the new forest, which fuels a week-long regrowth. Stella (now ninth-stage Soulfire) and Diana (eighth-stage) are safe at his side and run the sect's affairs; the oath-bound Redclaw family administers Darklight City; Larry and Maple are drained but presumed alive. Diana delivers Douglas, the sect's first hired builder. Open threads: the Dao Storm's true origin and why it hunted Ashlock; the Celestial Empire's World Tree, now blocked from ascending and a future danger; the eight remaining divine fragments; the looming beast tide; the real Patriarch's eventual reaction; and whether Ashlock's humanity will fade.
in_short: A man from Earth is reborn as a sentient, immobile demonic tree named Ashlock, rooted at a mountain peak and fed corpses by a fierce young cultivator, Stella Crestfallen. Growing through a private reward system, he survives assassins, a rival house's war, and a lightning tribulation, gathering companions: the squirrel Maple, the spider Larry, and eventually Diana Ravenborne, a homeless enemy heir turned ally. He forms a soul core, is revealed to possibly be a nascent 'World Tree,' and founds the Ashfallen sect. A mysterious elder, Senior Lee, gives him a divine fragment that rockets him to the Star Core realm and demi-divine status. After repelling a massive siege and subjugating the Redclaw family, Ashlock faces a sentient Dao Storm that destroys his body. He survives by permanently merging his human mind with the tree, learns the Nine Realms cosmology from Senior Lee, and, regrowing amid a new forest, resolves to surpass the Patriarch, endure the coming beast tide, and one day face the empire's World Tree.

recaps.text: A man from Earth wakes to find he has been reborn as a demonic spirit tree, rooted in a mountaintop courtyard and unable to move, speak, or be seen. He is fully aware, feeds on corpses through an innate Devour ability, and grows stronger by 'signing in' to a private status system that hands him new skills. A blonde young cultivator, later named Stella Crestfallen, defends the peak and feeds him the bodies of those she kills; in return he gifts her a pair of cursed fear-inducing earrings. She then wins a tournament to keep her home, killing a House Ravenborne scion in the match. Ashlock quietly saves her from poisoners and an assassination raid ordered by House Ravenborne, making his first direct kills. A powerful sect Grand Elder, an old friend of Stella's dead father, inspects the peak, discovers Ashlock is intelligent, and chooses to keep it secret. He also delivers hard news: House Ravenborne wants the peak as compensation, and the sect Patriarch has named Stella heir on the condition she pass a Grand Elder examination within five years or forfeit everything. Stella resolves to train harder, and Ashlock decides to stop sleeping his life away.

A violent storm nearly kills Ashlock: lightning shatters his branches and sets him burning from within, and Stella climbs into the storm to shield him, even taking strikes herself. He heals slowly inside an elaborate energy-channeling formation she builds, emerging months later far larger and stronger, having gained fire and lightning resistances. Attempting a costly high-grade summon, Ashlock instead draws a small white squirrel through a closing rift; it forms a mutual pact with him and Stella, who names it Maple. Intelligent and able to teleport with no visible power, Maple can relay Ashlock's simple yes-or-no wishes to Stella, giving them their first crude bridge. Now about fifteen, Stella has built her cultivation around a 'Tao of Lightning' and deliberately avoids the corrupting shortcuts other demonic cultivators use. With three years left before her examination, she departs for roughly a year of training elsewhere, taking Maple and leaving Ashlock alone at the peak, one stage short of forming his own soul core.

With Stella away, two House Ravenborne intruders search the peak for a missing kinsman's corpse. Ashlock manipulates the pair into turning on each other, and the survivor is revealed as Diana Ravenborne, forced into being her family's heir after Stella killed her brother. Diana's father, the Ravenborne Grand Elder, orders her to assassinate Stella within five years or die. Before she can act, a three-house war erupts: Houses Evergreen and Winterwrath jointly attack House Ravenborne over territory and its spirit-stone mines, devastating the nearby town of Darklight City. Cornered and defeated, the Ravenborne Grand Elder, secretly far stronger than he appeared, unleashes a suicidal 'supernova' that obliterates his own peak and much of the city; the blast knocks Ashlock unconscious. Amid the chaos, Tristan Evergreen murders his cousin Wayne and slips away to hunt the Grand Elder's disembodied soul. Homeless and hunted, Diana flees to the now-empty Red Vine Peak to hide, unaware the ominous tree there is anything more than it seems.

Ashlock warily tolerates Diana as she recovers and quietly investigates him. When an intruder named Mike arrives hunting Stella for a horrific 'pill furnace' scheme, Ashlock and Diana kill him together, and Ashlock absorbs his soul core to finally form his own, an amethyst core of the rare Spatial element, ascending into the Soulfire realm and surviving a brutal lightning tribulation that leaves him a full-grown tree. Stella returns, mistakes Diana for an assassin, and fights her, but the two reconcile once Diana explains her family is destroyed and she bears Stella no ill will. Stella at last confirms Ashlock's sentience directly, establishing a flame-flicker yes-or-no signal, and privately admits she misjudged Diana. Diana gives up her risky beast-core cultivation at Stella's urging, and the two become allied training partners preparing for the Grand Elder examination, with Diana barred from further contact with Ashlock.

Ashlock summons a car-sized spider he names Larry, bonded to him by a mental tether. Seeing his rift-opening, corpse-controlling, and communicating powers, Diana theorizes that Ashlock may be a nascent 'World Tree,' a legendary being whose birth would herald the end of the current era and mark him for death by both merchants and the Celestial Empire. Committing to protect him, Stella and Diana train intensely. Larry becomes a feared night hunter of cultivators, and Ashlock secretly breeds poisoned fruit that seeds demonic-tree offspring around the mountain. Straining to communicate, Ashlock finally spells out his first written message, 'I am Ashlock,' using a puppeted corpse and blood, in a script so ancient that Stella and Diana cannot read it. Determined to learn it herself, Stella sets off with Diana to find a teacher, while Larry begins a mysterious evolution.

In Darklight City, Stella learns the true stakes behind her upbringing: the Patriarch protects her only because he intends one day to use her as a living 'pill furnace.' At the Academy library she studies the ancient script well enough to grasp Ashlock's message. She and Diana also learn that a massive beast tide is coming within a few years, that the Blood Lotus sect already plans to abandon its depleted territory and relocate, and that the houses expect to fight over the best new land, with most mortals left to die. Larry's ongoing rampage has made him a local legend, the 'Ashen Devourer,' blamed for slaughtering cultivators near the villages. Before heading home, the pair trade with traveling merchants for new clothing and a spatial technique, and Stella turns aside a merchant's attempt to seize her cursed earrings.

Back at Red Vine Peak, Ashlock reestablishes written contact and reveals his goal: to found a sect. Stella and Diana join him, and he names it Ashfallen. Diana explains how beast tides work, driven by energy flowing along ley lines and pooling at 'spiritual springs' that deplete and force beasts to migrate straight through settled land. Weighing whether to fight, flee, or shelter, Ashlock reveals he has already hollowed a long root-tunnel down to an abandoned mine he can use as a refuge. He also undergoes a painful mutation that opens a 'Demonic Eye' in his trunk, an eye that lays bare a person's soul core and floods anyone it targets with terror, even seasoned cultivators. Diana makes the first trip down the tunnel as her opening task for the sect, reaching the mine and glimpsing what looks like an inhabited town deeper within. The founding trio begins turning the neglected peak and its mine into the seed of a real sect.

Ashlock extends deeper into the mine, naming the captured slime Bob, and learns to cast spatial techniques by piloting corpses to supply the hands he lacks. Then a mysterious elder called Senior Lee arrives, radiating no power yet wielding effortless spatial magic. He diagnoses that Stella damaged her own spirit root by cultivating too fast, gives her medicine to slow the decay, and, uniquely, speaks with Ashlock telepathically, treating him as an equal. Lee gifts Ashlock a rare 'Divine Fragment' that violently rockets his cultivation toward the Star Core realm and triggers a colossal tribulation. When Stella is struck down protecting him, Lee revives her with a divine pill, then flees the heavens' wrath for having done so. As the tribulation blazes, hundreds of Evergreen and Winterwrath cultivators, led by Tristan (now Star Core), storm Red Vine Peak, believing Stella is the one ascending.

The siege is fought off in waves, with Larry, Diana's mist, Stella's blade, and Ashlock's Devour vines cutting down attacker after attacker. Tristan latches onto Ashlock's forming star core to drain it, but Stella and Ashlock's manifested Demonic Eye bring him down, and he is killed and consumed. Ashlock completes his Star Core ascension, then merges with the Divine Fragment to become a 'Demonic Demi-Divine Tree,' now able to open spatial portals directly through his own body and pick off enemies at range. Diana suffers a violent relapse of her long-buried 'heart demons.' Larry cocoons and re-emerges as the 'Ashen King,' a far more powerful being. Meanwhile, an unseen entity massacres the leaders of both invading houses at their new palace, leaving Eleanor Evergreen to flee with a garbled, terrified warning.

The transformed Larry, now able to speak, wipes out the remaining Winterwrath and Evergreen forces overnight. Ashlock pushes into Star Core stage two and gains a puzzling 'Mystic Realm' skill of unclear purpose. Diana purges her heart-demon corruption by channeling it into Bob during endless sparring, finally breaking her years-long bottleneck. When the red-haired Redclaw family comes to investigate the destruction, Stella and Larry cow them with overwhelming force and a fabricated legend of a hidden 'immortal ancestor,' binding them by oath into the Ashfallen sect. Ashlock masters telekinesis, at last writing to Stella and Diana without needing a corpse. Then he activates the untested Mystic Realm, and everyone but him vanishes into a strange white fog.

The Mystic Realm scatters Stella, Diana, Larry, and Maple into separate, time-dilated pocket worlds, each rich with a different kind of energy; after a week of local time, all return safely and much stronger. Ashlock learns the ability has a long cooldown and can drive versatile growth across his followers. The Redclaw family moves fully into the White Stone Palace and begins openly administering Darklight City on Ashfallen's behalf, swearing binding loyalty oaths and organizing a public alchemy tournament to recruit an outside alchemist. Both Stella and Diana break through to new Soulfire stages, nearing the Star Core realm, and begin quietly managing the sect's city business in disguise, while Ashlock keeps his true nature and full strength hidden even from his new subordinates.

Ashlock gains a sinister new mutation, 'Blood Sap,' whose black fluid can slowly turn anyone who drinks it into a tree. Then a sentient Dao Storm, a titan-like vortex of water, wind, and lightning, descends on the region and advances straight for Ashlock. He portals the Redclaw family and nearby villagers into the mine for shelter, then fights the storm alongside Larry and the Redclaw Grand Elder, but nothing they do can stop it; it shatters his barriers and burns through his bark. As Ashlock's power fails, Maple abruptly reappears and cleaves the storm's humanoid core cleanly in two before collapsing, spent. It is not enough. The storm knits itself back together, engulfs the mountain, and tears Ashlock's tree body apart, his cursed black sap spraying into the vortex as he is destroyed.

The Dao Storm dissipates, raining 'Black Rain,' bark fragments that instantly sprout thousands of demonic trees across Red Vine Peak and Darklight City. Ashlock is reduced to a charred stump, his consciousness nearly gone. Stella saves him by pouring her own cultivation energy into his dying core. His rebooted system offers a choice: free his human ego to be reborn separately, or fully merge it into the tree. Ashlock chooses to merge permanently, insisting he is a tree now; the merge costs him his discrete star core, whose power now suffuses his whole trunk, and comes with a warning that his human traits will erode over time. In a vision, a youthful Senior Lee reveals the wider cosmology: the collapsing Nine Realms, nine scattered divine fragments, and an existing Monarch-realm World Tree seated at the center of the Celestial Empire, now permanently blocked from ascending because Ashlock holds the fragment meant for it, making it a distant future threat that could seek to consume him. Lee bids farewell, saying his own time is ending. Ashlock wakes linked to the new forest, which fuels his regrowth over about a week. Diana arrives with Douglas, the sect's first hired builder, and Ashlock resolves to grow strong enough to surpass the Patriarch, survive the coming beast tide, and one day confront the empire's World Tree, while cherishing the found family at his side.
